purpose: settings.parallel_computing=true on a THREE-rule program with interacting heads (two writers of popular — popular_rule and echo_rule — plus trendy_rule consuming it, cyclically): the rules-loop prange at interpretation_parallel.py:571 runs at width 3, the multi-rule class the one-rule parallel pair deliberately left unexercised. Screened at the pin (session 31, warm kernel cache, PYTHONHASHSEED=0): 4 fresh oracle processes digest-identical (deterministic), and every reasoning probe digest-equaled the serial default engine's on the identical program AND on a rule-order permutation of it — the width-3 prange did not reorder trace appends or updates relative to the serial engine on this program. Rule-list order itself IS behavior (permuting it moves a same-timestep duplicate derivation's trace attribution to the other writing rule and reorders equal-bound rows in the filtered frames), but identically in both engines, so this case pins the base order only. The rewrite's parallel knob runs the single default core (ADR 0003 / batch B11), which is exactly what the oracle's parallel kernel computed here.
provenance: session-31 harness-quality packet: screened characterization of the pinned parallel kernel's multi-rule prange, promoted to a case per the deterministic/stable/coverage-adding gate; extends docs/surface.md setting:parallel_computing beyond the one-rule family
